analysis.consistency: Consistent with typical D&D 5e feature presentation for class abilities.
analysis.quality: High quality. Clearly states the level gained, the core mechanic (gaining Metamagic options), and the limitation (only one Metamagic per spell).

mechanics.trigger: Gained at 3rd level (initial), then at 10th and 17th level.
mechanics.effect: Grants the Sorcerer access to Metamagic options.
mechanics.details: The initial grant is 2 options. Subsequent grants add 1 more option each time. The fundamental rule that only one Metamagic can be applied per casting is clearly stated.

issues: The feature name 'Metamagic' is descriptive but very generic; however, this is standard nomenclature for this ability in D&D.
The RAW does not list the available options, which is expected as this feature grants access to a list defined elsewhere.
